Clinical Notes
The patient's persistent stage 2 hypertension (BP >160/100 mmHg) despite a maximum dose of an ACE inhibitor (Lisinopril 40mg) necessitates
augmentation of her antihypertensive regimen. Given her diabetes and the recommendation from her prior visit, Chlorthalidone 25mg daily is chosen
as the next agent due to its proven efficacy in reducing cardiovascular events and favorable profile in diabetic patients with hypertension. While
Amlodipine was also considered, Chlorthalidone offers a different mechanism of action which can be synergistic with Lisinopril, and its long half-life
supports once-daily dosing. Secondary causes of hypertension, such as renal artery stenosis or primary aldosteronism, remain in the differential for
resistant hypertension, warranting further investigation if blood pressure remains uncontrolled after medication optimization.
